what_it_does: Renders a motion-animated disclosure card (panel, sheet, or rail) for a selected projection point. It fetches a context bundle, explains in plain words why the point sits where it does, humanizes machine-facing context text, and offers lens-specific footer actions such as re-centring or setting separator poles.
observations: Uses AnimatePresence and motion.aside with variant-driven enter/exit animations.
Fetches context via contextAt(addr) inside useEffect with a live flag for cancellation.
Defines placement() to translate binding.radius_from into human-readable rows for semantic, separator, nucleation, address, and fallback age lenses.
humanizeCtx strips composed address frames, bare scheme URLs, provenance tails, absolute paths, and machine id tokens at the render boundary.
Comments repeatedly enforce an operator-law: show human registry names (sector_name, kind_name, kind_meaning), never raw machine ids or addresses.
Caps displayed context items at six and surfaces a +more count.
Footer conditionally exposes pole buttons only when binding.radius_from is separator and onSetPole is provided.
standouts: The file is as much a domain interpreter as a UI component: placement() maps projection geometry into plain-language explanations.
An explicit render-time scrubber (humanizeCtx) removes brain-facing addresses and provenance while leaving the underlying context text intact.
Heavy inline commentary codifies a human-vs-machine text contract across the component.
conventions: React functional component with hooks and TypeScript prop types.
Framer Motion for entrance/exit transitions driven by a motion feel token.
BEM-ish CSS class names scoped to disclosure variants.
useEffect cleanup via a boolean live flag.
Default parameter values and early returns.
concerns: humanizeCtx relies on several regexes whose conservative claims are not locally verifiable; legitimate prose containing colons, slashes, @, or hashes could be stripped.
No loading state is shown while the context bundle is being fetched; only error, empty, or item states appear.
placement() embeds hardcoded distance thresholds (0.34, 0.67) and presentation wording inside the component.
Tone class strings born/strain/pile are magic values without local type safety.
pointAddress is imported but its implementation and stability guarantees are not visible in this file.
notes: A surface-level interpreter that turns a selected wheel point and its projection binding into operator-readable prose, while scrubbing machine-facing context for human display.
questions: What is the full shape of ContextBundle and ProjPoint beyond the fields used here?
How are sector_name, kind_name, and kind_meaning populated and kept in sync with the registry?
What testing exists to ensure humanizeCtx does not remove meaningful operator prose?
How is the MotionFeel token system defined and what values can feel take?
Is there an intended retry or refresh behaviour when contextAt throws?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is explicitly a human-readable disclosure for a selected point and does what the comments describe.
apparent_themes: Render-boundary translation rather than source mutation.
Registry-true human names versus machine identifiers.
intention_signals: The file is trying to be a readable why-is-this-here explainer for a complex projection lens, keeping the operator in plain language while preserving machine references for downstream systems.
novelty: The explicit operator-law contract and a dedicated render-time humanizer that strips brain-facing addresses and provenance while leaving source text intact is unusual for a UI component.
contribution: A localized, comment-heavy interpreter that maps projection geometry and context into human prose, plus a render-time scrubber for machine-facing metadata.
summary_for_embedding: Disclosure.tsx is a React component that renders an animated panel, sheet, or rail explaining a selected projection point's sector, radius meaning, and humanized context items, with lens-specific footer actions.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to give operators a plain-language reading of why a point appears where it does in a wheel projection without exposing machine identifiers or raw addresses.
suggested_fields: radius_from_interpretation_rules — a structured ledger field for the mapping from binding.radius_from to human-readable rows and thresholds.
operator_law_contracts — a field capturing files that enforce human-readable surface versus machine-facing source separation.
proposed_edge_kinds: translates_for — between a surface component and the machine-facing data it renders into human prose.
scrubs_at_boundary — between raw context text and its rendered humanized form.

symbols.does: Defines the three disclosure layout variants.
symbols.description: A union type of panel, sheet, and rail. It selects the component's layout mode and drives both the CSS class suffix and the direction of the Framer Motion enter/exit animation.

symbols.does: Shapes a single interpreted placement row.
symbols.description: Describes one key/value pair used to explain a point's position, optionally carrying a numeric value and a tone class such as born, strain, or pile for visual emphasis.

symbols.does: Returns the last path segment of an identifier.
symbols.description: Strips trailing slashes and yields the final component of a slash-delimited string, used to turn machine paths like code://projections/worldview.py into the readable leaf worldview.py.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/wheel/Disclosure.tsx::placement | function | function placement(p: ProjPoint, binding: Projection['binding'] | undefined, centre: string | null): Place[] {
symbols.does: Builds human-readable rows explaining a point's sector and radius meaning.
symbols.description: Inspects the point's sector_name, scale clustering, and the projection binding's radius_from to produce plain-language rows such as meaning distance, lean, fit, filing distance, or age, each paired with the real numeric value.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/wheel/Disclosure.tsx::num | function | const num = (x: number | undefined) => (typeof x === 'number' ? x.toFixed(2) : '—')
symbols.does: Formats a number to two decimals or an em-dash.
symbols.description: A small local formatter used throughout placement() to present numeric values consistently when they are defined.

symbols.does: Strips machine addresses and provenance from context text at render time.
symbols.description: Uses regex replacements to remove composed address frames, bare scheme URLs, provenance tails with hashes and absolute paths, and machine id tokens, leaving operator-facing prose while the underlying text remains unchanged.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/wheel/Disclosure.tsx::excerpt | function | function excerpt(s: string, max = 150): string {
symbols.does: Truncates text on a word boundary with an ellipsis.
symbols.description: Collapses whitespace and, if over the max length, cuts at the last space before the limit and appends an ellipsis so the summary never breaks mid-word.

symbols.does: Renders the animated disclosure card for a selected point.
symbols.description: Fetches the context bundle for the selected point's address, then renders the header, kind meaning, summary, placement rows, humanized context items, and conditional footer actions for setting separator poles or re-centring the view.
